findings.problem: The middle escalation repeats the same negotiate-then-refuse beat across kitchen and court scenes (continue training vs stop), which slows momentum before the late-night collapse and weakens leverage progression.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten and differentiate confrontation beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:197, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:231, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:281 so each exchange introduces new irreversible leverage (for example: one scene sets policy, the next enforces consequence) instead of repeating ask/deny variants.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if revised versions of the spans around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:197 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:231 each contain one unique leverage shift that did not exist in the prior beat, and the ask/deny loop does not recur again in the span around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:281; fail if the same continue-vs-stop argument repeats without new consequence.

findings.problem: Anika's numeric stress-tell (pain scoring/counting) is used so frequently across scenes that it starts reading like checklist reuse from the style profile rather than evolving scene-specific behavior, reducing emotional texture.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep explicit numeric pain reporting where medically or strategically necessary (nurse assessment at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:103), but in later beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:261, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:319, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:371 shift at least two updates into embodied consequences, misdirection, or action that changes the scene's power dynamic.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after the nurse scene near snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:103, at least two later pain-status moments in the spans around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:261, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:319, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:371 are conveyed without explicit numeric labels and still make severity unambiguous; fail if those beats continue as repeated number declarations.

findings.problem: Reaction-beat defaults repeat past the tic threshold (especially repeated nodding, plus repeated looked-at staging), flattening paragraph-level surprise and signaling autopilot gesture writing.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:35, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:71, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:149, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:299, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:333, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:363, replace most nod-based reactions with distinct physical or interior responses tied to immediate stakes; also diversify looked-at beats near snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:101 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:215.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the cited reaction spans retain no more than two total nodding beats and no more than two total looked-at staging beats, with each replacement introducing a concrete action/sensation specific to that moment's conflict; fail if repetitive gesture placeholders remain the dominant reaction form in those lines.

summary: Causality, continuity, and tense control are solid, and character registers are largely consistent with the style bible. The chapter still fails review due to unresolved structural repetition in confrontation beats and prose-level repetition tics (gesture defaults and over-recycled numeric stress reporting) that reduce escalation sharpness and stylistic freshness.
